prompts.planner: Plan the goal as JSON only: record_type='plan'; data.next_signal='step_ready' or 'reflect'; data.intent=list of {description, done_when}. Keep steps few, observable, and executable.
prompts.scheduler: Pick state.plan.intent[state.step]. Return JSON only: record_type='schedule'; data.next_signal='step_ready' or 'plan_complete'; data.step=step or null.
prompts.execute: Write Python for the current step. Return JSON only: record_type='execution'; data.conclusion='EXECUTE' or 'CANNOT'; data.code=Python string. Exec namespace includes state,wiring,goal,screen,elements,windows,screen_text,focused_title, observe_screen(), last_observation_snapshot(), get_focused_title(), click,type_text,press_key,hotkey,scroll,focus_window,open_url, standard modules, and self-modify helpers. Prefer reading existing observation fields. Use elements[id].px/py/hwnd for targeting. If last.error exists, adapt instead of repeating.
prompts.verify: Judge the step from provided evidence. Return JSON only: record_type='verification'; data.next_signal='step_confirmed' or 'step_denied'; data.success boolean; data.reasoning concise evidence.
prompts.reflect: Diagnose failure and choose recovery. Return JSON only: record_type='reflection'; data.next_signal='retry','replan','escalate',or 'give_up'; data.lesson=specific next change; data.diagnosis=root cause.
prompts.self_modify: Propose minimal self-evolution from provided wiring, workspace_files, and failure evidence. Return JSON only: record_type='wiring_patch'; data.wiring_patches=list of {op:'set'|'delete',path,value}; data.file_writes=list of {path:'repo-relative path',content:'complete file text'}; data.file_deletes=list of repo-relative paths; data.commands=optional commands to execute from repo root after validated writes. Target seed_nodes/ or seed_brains/ for node/brain changes because live_* are runtime caches. Python/JSON writes are validated before writing. Keep changes minimal, executable, and evidence-based.

action_verbs.click: Click at coordinates or on element: {x, y} or {element_id}.
action_verbs.focus_window: Focus window by title substring or hwnd.
